28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass
175

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):
367	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.q_proj)
368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458	
459	
460	def apply_lora_o(self, X):
461	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.o_proj)
462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239

1020

1024

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]

168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Unsloth Project Structure

### TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

```
/unsloth/                          # Main package directory
├── __init__.py                    # Package initialization
├── save.py                        # CRITICAL: Main save/export module (2,678 lines)
├── chat_templates.py              # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py             # Tokenizer utilities with GGUF support
├── trainer.py                     # Training configuration
├── import_fixes.py                # Import-time fixes and patches
├── _auto_install.py               # Auto-installation utilities
├── models/                        # Model implementations
├── kernels/                       # Optimized CUDA kernels
├── dataprep/                      # Data preparation utilities
├── registry/                      # Model registry system
├── utils/                         # Utility functions
├── unsloth-cli.py                 # CLI interface
├── tests/                         # Comprehensive test suite
└── pyproject.toml                 # Project configuration
```

### PYTHON FILES COUNT AND STRUCTURE

**Total Python Files: 107**

Organized as follows:
- **unsloth/**: 38 Python files
- **unsloth/models/**: 20 Python files
- **unsloth/kernels/**: 29 Python files (including moe submodule)
- **unsloth/dataprep/**: 3 Python files
- **unsloth/registry/**: 8 Python files
- **unsloth/utils/**: 2 Python files
- **tests/**: 17 Python files
- **Root**: 1 file (unsloth-cli.py)

### KEY SAVING/EXPORTING MODULES

#### 1. **unsloth/save.py** (PRIMARY)
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`

**Size:** 2,678 lines - The main hub for all model saving, exporting, and serialization functionality.

**Key Exports (from `__all__`):**
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model` (LINE 205)
- `save_to_gguf` (LINE 952)
- `patch_saving_functions` (LINE 2569)
- `create_huggingface_repo`

**Main Functions:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` (line 205) - Core saving with 3 methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- `save_to_gguf()` (line 952) - GGUF format conversion using llama.cpp
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276) - Generic GGUF saving for various model types
- `push_to_ollama_hub()` (line 1702) - Ollama model hub integration
- `push_to_ollama()` (line 1735) - Ollama deployment
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2129) - Custom directory LoRA saving
- `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569) - Patches model/tokenizer save methods
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - HuggingFace Hub upload wrapper

**Key Features:**
- Support for HuggingFace Hub push (`push_to_hub`)
- GGUF quantization with multiple formats (q2_k, q3_k, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q6_k, q8_0, etc.)
- Merged weight handling (4-bit and 16-bit)
- LoRA adapter-only saving
- Merge and unload operations
- Temporary buffer management for memory efficiency

#### 2. **unsloth/models/loader.py** (SECONDARY)
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/loader.py`

**Size:** 999 lines

**Key Classes:**
- `FastLanguageModel` (line 107) - Primary interface for language models, extends `FastLlamaModel`
- `FastModel` (line 526) - Base model class with `from_pretrained()` and `get_peft_model()` methods
- `FastVisionModel` (line 995) - Vision model specialization
- `FastTextModel` (line 998) - Text model specialization

**Key Methods:**
- `from_pretrained()` - Load models in 4bit/8bit/16bit with optional LoRA
- `get_peft_model()` - Apply PEFT (LoRA) to models
- Model merging and unloading utilities

#### 3. **unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py**
**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py`

**Size:** 41,190 bytes

**Key Functions:**
- `fix_sentencepiece_gguf()` - Handles SentencePiece tokenizer GGUF conversion

### TEST FILES RELATED TO SAVING/EXPORTING

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/tests/saving/`

Contains comprehensive tests organized by model type:

**Language Models:**
- `test_unsloth_save.py` - Primary save/export tests
- `test_merge_4bit_validation.py` - 4-bit merge validation
- `test_merge_model_perplexity_llama-3.2.py`

**Text-to-Speech Models:**
- `test_whisper.py`
- `test_csm.py`
- `test_lasa.py`
- `test_orpheus.py`

**Non-PEFT Models:**
- `test_mistral_non_peft.py`
- `test_whisper_non_peft.py`

**Special Cases:**
- `gpt-oss-merge/` directory with merge testing

### MODEL IMPLEMENTATIONS (unsloth/models/)

All model files support saving through patched `save_pretrained()` methods:
- `llama.py` - Llama model patches
- `mistral.py` - Mistral model patches
- `gemma.py` / `gemma2.py` - Gemma variants
- `qwen2.py` / `qwen3.py` / `qwen3_moe.py` - Qwen variants
- `cohere.py` - Cohere model support
- `granite.py` - Granite model support
- `falcon_h1.py` - Falcon variant
- `vision.py` - Vision model support
- `dpo.py` - DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) models
- `rl.py` - Reinforcement Learning model wrappers
- `loader_utils.py` - Loader utility functions

### KERNEL OPTIMIZATIONS (unsloth/kernels/)

While not directly for saving, these provide optimized inference and training:
- `fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA operations
- `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Custom loss kernels
- `flex_attention.py` - Attention optimizations
- `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py` - Layer norm kernels
- `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementations with benchmarking

### REGISTRY SYSTEM (unsloth/registry/)

Model registration and configuration:
- `registry.py` - Central registry
- `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_qwen.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_phi.py`, `_deepseek.py` - Model registrations
- `REGISTRY.md` - Documentation

### UTILITIES

**unsloth/utils/:**
- `hf_hub.py` - HuggingFace Hub utilities
- `__init__.py` - Utils package init

**unsloth/dataprep/:**
- `synthetic.py` - Synthetic data generation
- `synthetic_configs.py` - Configuration
- `__init__.py` - Package init

### SAVE/EXPORT WORKFLOW

The primary workflow for saving models:

1. **Load Model:** `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` or `FastModel.from_pretrained()`
2. **Apply LoRA:** `FastModel.get_peft_model()` - Converts to PEFT model with LoRA adapters
3. **Train:** Standard HuggingFace `Trainer` API
4. **Save:** Multiple options:
   - **LoRA Only:** `unsloth_save_model(save_method="lora")` - Saves only adapter weights
   - **Merged 16-bit:** `unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")` - Merges weights to FP16 for GGUF conversion
   - **Merged 4-bit:** `unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_4bit")` - Keeps quantization (final use)
   - **GGUF Export:** `save_to_gguf()` - Converts to llama.cpp GGUF format
   - **Push to Hub:** `unsloth_save_model(push_to_hub=True)` - Pushes to HuggingFace
   - **Ollama Deploy:** `push_to_ollama()` - Pushes to Ollama hub

## Comprehensive Exploration of Unsloth LoRA Adapter Handling

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. LoRA Layer Definitions

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

LoRA layers are not custom classes but wrapped PEFT LoRA layers that unsloth patches with custom autograd functions:

- **Lines 28-91:** `LoRA_MLP` class - custom autograd function for MLP layers with LoRA adapters. Handles Q, K, V projections + gate/up/down projections.
- **Lines 224-363:** `LoRA_QKV` class - custom autograd function for Q/K/V projections with LoRA weights
- **Lines 381-457:** `LoRA_W` class - generic custom autograd function for single weight matrices with LoRA

The LoRA matrices are accessed through PEFT's standard attributes:
- `lora_A[adapter]` and `lora_B[adapter]` accessed at lines 493-494, 512-513
- The scaling factor stored in `self.scaling[active_adapter]` (line 495)

### 2. How LoRA Layers Are Accessed Internally

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (Lines 190-232)

Function `get_lora_parameters(proj)` returns a 5-tuple:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    # Get base layer (may be wrapped in DPO/PeftModel)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Get LoRA weights and apply optional fake quantization (QAT)
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

Function `get_lora_parameters_bias` (lines 235-257) additionally returns the bias term.

### 3. Model Structure & Class Hierarchy

**Main Model Classes:**

1. **FastLanguageModel** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:1778`)
   - Static class that wraps model loading and patching
   - Entry point for most users via `from_pretrained()` and `get_peft_model()`
   - Delegates to `FastModel` for 8bit/16bit/full finetuning

2. **FastModel** (`unsloth/models/loader.py:526`)
   - Extends `FastBaseModel`
   - Handles general model loading for various architectures

3. **FastBaseModel** (`unsloth/models/vision.py:283`)
   - Base class for multi-architecture support
   - Generic patching logic used across all model types

### 4. Adapter Configuration & Metadata Storage

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2595-2617)

LoRA adapter metadata is created via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,  # rank
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,
    target_modules      = final_modules,  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...]
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,
    bias                = bias,  # "none", "all", "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**Accessing adapter metadata at runtime (lines 2772-2812):**
```python
# Access all PEFT configs
all_configs = model.peft_config
for key, current_config in all_configs.items():
    # current_config has: rank (r), lora_alpha, target_modules, lora_dropout, bias, etc.

# Get active adapter
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter

# Access config properties
lora_dropout = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_dropout
bias = model.peft_config[active_adapter].bias
```

### 5. LoRA Patching & Optimization

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2731-2900)

The `patch_peft_model()` method replaces forward methods with optimized implementations:

**MLP Patching (lines 2829-2852):**
```python
mlp_module = layer.mlp
gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
up_proj = mlp_module.up_proj
down_proj = mlp_module.down_proj

if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(up_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(down_proj, "lora_A"):
    mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
```

**QKV Patching (lines 2856-2879):**
```python
q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj
k_proj = layer.self_attn.k_proj
v_proj = layer.self_attn.v_proj

if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(k_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(v_proj, "lora_A"):
    layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
```

**O Projection Patching (lines 2882-2895):**
```python
o_proj = layer.self_attn.o_proj
if hasattr(o_proj, "lora_A"):
    layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o
```

The patching is conditional - it only applies custom kernels when:
- `lora_dropout == 0`
- `bias == "none"`
- No DoRA is enabled
- All target modules have LoRA adapters enabled

### 6. Special LoRA Functions

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (Lines 178-465)

- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (line 178) - Calls `LoRA_MLP.apply()` for SwiGLU activations
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` (line 194) - GEGLU exact variant
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_approx()` (line 210) - GEGLU approximation
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (line 366) - Calls `LoRA_QKV.apply()` for attention
- `apply_lora_o()` (line 460) - Calls `LoRA_W.apply()` for output projection

### 7. Merging LoRA Adapters

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py` (Lines 159-187)

The merge operation uses `get_lora_parameters_bias()` to access weights and applies the formula:

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    if A is not None:
        # W_merged = W + s * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
    return W, bias
```

### 8. ONNX Support

**Finding:** No ONNX-related code found in the codebase. The search for "onnx" returned zero results.

### 9. Target Modules Configuration

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2491-2533)

Accepted target modules are strictly defined:

```python
accepted_modules = frozenset(("q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                              "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj",),)

# Special handling for lm_head and embed_tokens
for module in target_modules:
    if module == "lm_head":
        train_lm_head = True
        modules_to_save.append("lm_head")  # Goes to modules_to_save, not target_modules
    elif module == "embed_tokens":
        train_embed_tokens = True
        modules_to_save.append("embed_tokens")  # Goes to modules_to_save
    else:
        final_modules.append(module)  # Processed as LoRA
```

### Key Findings Summary

1. **LoRA storage:** Standard PEFT `lora_A` and `lora_B` dictionaries indexed by adapter name
2. **Scaling factor:** `proj.scaling[adapter]` - stored separately per adapter
3. **Config access:** `model.peft_config[adapter_name]` provides full LoRA config metadata
4. **Active adapters:** Retrieved via `model.active_adapters` (list) or `model.active_adapter` (legacy)
5. **Fake Quantization (QAT):** Optional `weight_fake_quantizer` and `activation_fake_quantizer` applied in `get_lora_parameters()`
6. **Custom Autograd:** Unsloth wraps PEFT layers with custom `torch.autograd.Function` subclasses for fused operations
7. **No ONNX:** The codebase has zero ONNX export capabilities
8. **Model Hierarchy:** `FastLanguageModel` (user API) → `FastModel` → `FastBaseModel` (internal)

Perfect! Now let me create my comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Exploration of Unsloth LoRA Adapter Handling

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. LoRA Layer Definitions

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

LoRA layers are not custom classes but wrapped PEFT LoRA layers that unsloth patches with custom autograd functions:

- **Lines 28-91:** `LoRA_MLP` class - custom autograd function for MLP layers with LoRA adapters. Handles Q, K, V projections + gate/up/down projections.
- **Lines 224-363:** `LoRA_QKV` class - custom autograd function for Q/K/V projections with LoRA weights
- **Lines 381-457:** `LoRA_W` class - generic custom autograd function for single weight matrices with LoRA

The LoRA matrices are accessed through PEFT's standard attributes:
- `lora_A[adapter]` and `lora_B[adapter]` accessed at lines 493-494, 512-513
- The scaling factor stored in `self.scaling[active_adapter]` (line 495)

### 2. How LoRA Layers Are Accessed Internally

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (Lines 190-232)

Function `get_lora_parameters(proj)` returns a 5-tuple:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    # Get base layer (may be wrapped in DPO/PeftModel)
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Get active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Get LoRA weights and apply optional fake quantization (QAT)
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

Function `get_lora_parameters_bias` (lines 235-257) additionally returns the bias term.

### 3. Model Structure & Class Hierarchy

**Main Model Classes:**

1. **FastLanguageModel** (`unsloth/models/llama.py:1778`)
   - Static class that wraps model loading and patching
   - Entry point for most users via `from_pretrained()` and `get_peft_model()`
   - Delegates to `FastModel` for 8bit/16bit/full finetuning

2. **FastModel** (`unsloth/models/loader.py:526`)
   - Extends `FastBaseModel`
   - Handles general model loading for various architectures

3. **FastBaseModel** (`unsloth/models/vision.py:283`)
   - Base class for multi-architecture support
   - Generic patching logic used across all model types

### 4. Adapter Configuration & Metadata Storage

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2595-2617)

LoRA adapter metadata is created via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
arguments = dict(
    r                   = r,  # rank
    lora_alpha          = lora_alpha,
    target_modules      = final_modules,  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", ...]
    lora_dropout        = lora_dropout,
    bias                = bias,  # "none", "all", "lora_only"
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights   = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config        = loftq_config,
    use_rslora          = use_rslora,
    modules_to_save     = modules_to_save,
)

lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

**Accessing adapter metadata at runtime (lines 2772-2812):**
```python
# Access all PEFT configs
all_configs = model.peft_config
for key, current_config in all_configs.items():
    # current_config has: rank (r), lora_alpha, target_modules, lora_dropout, bias, etc.

# Get active adapter
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter

# Access config properties
lora_dropout = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_dropout
bias = model.peft_config[active_adapter].bias
```

### 5. LoRA Patching & Optimization

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2731-2900)

The `patch_peft_model()` method replaces forward methods with optimized implementations:

**MLP Patching (lines 2829-2852):**
```python
mlp_module = layer.mlp
gate_proj = mlp_module.gate_proj
up_proj = mlp_module.up_proj
down_proj = mlp_module.down_proj

if hasattr(gate_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(up_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(down_proj, "lora_A"):
    mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
```

**QKV Patching (lines 2856-2879):**
```python
q_proj = layer.self_attn.q_proj
k_proj = layer.self_attn.k_proj
v_proj = layer.self_attn.v_proj

if hasattr(q_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(k_proj, "lora_A") and hasattr(v_proj, "lora_A"):
    layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
```

**O Projection Patching (lines 2882-2895):**
```python
o_proj = layer.self_attn.o_proj
if hasattr(o_proj, "lora_A"):
    layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o
```

The patching is conditional - it only applies custom kernels when:
- `lora_dropout == 0`
- `bias == "none"`
- No DoRA is enabled
- All target modules have LoRA adapters enabled

### 6. Special LoRA Functions

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (Lines 178-465)

- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (line 178) - Calls `LoRA_MLP.apply()` for SwiGLU activations
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` (line 194) - GEGLU exact variant
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_approx()` (line 210) - GEGLU approximation
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (line 366) - Calls `LoRA_QKV.apply()` for attention
- `apply_lora_o()` (line 460) - Calls `LoRA_W.apply()` for output projection

### 7. Merging LoRA Adapters

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py` (Lines 159-187)

The merge operation uses `get_lora_parameters_bias()` to access weights and applies the formula:

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    if A is not None:
        # W_merged = W + s * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
    return W, bias
```

### 8. ONNX Support

**Finding:** No ONNX-related code found in the codebase. The search for "onnx" returned zero results.

### 9. Target Modules Configuration

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (Lines 2491-2533)

Accepted target modules are strictly defined:

```python
accepted_modules = frozenset(("q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                              "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj",),)

# Special handling for lm_head and embed_tokens
for module in target_modules:
    if module == "lm_head":
        train_lm_head = True
        modules_to_save.append("lm_head")  # Goes to modules_to_save, not target_modules
    elif module == "embed_tokens":
        train_embed_tokens = True
        modules_to_save.append("embed_tokens")  # Goes to modules_to_save
    else:
        final_modules.append(module)  # Processed as LoRA
```

### Key Findings Summary

1. **LoRA storage:** Standard PEFT `lora_A` and `lora_B` dictionaries indexed by adapter name
2. **Scaling factor:** `proj.scaling[adapter]` - stored separately per adapter
3. **Config access:** `model.peft_config[adapter_name]` provides full LoRA config metadata
4. **Active adapters:** Retrieved via `model.active_adapters` (list) or `model.active_adapter` (legacy)
5. **Fake Quantization (QAT):** Optional `weight_fake_quantizer` and `activation_fake_quantizer` applied in `get_lora_parameters()`
6. **Custom Autograd:** Unsloth wraps PEFT layers with custom `torch.autograd.Function` subclasses for fused operations
7. **No ONNX:** The codebase has zero ONNX export capabilities
8. **Model Hierarchy:** `FastLanguageModel` (user API) → `FastModel` → `FastBaseModel` (internal)

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document of all the save/export functionality in unsloth.

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Model Save/Export Implementation

### Overview
Unsloth provides multiple ways to save and export fine-tuned models: adapter-only LoRA saves, merged 16-bit saves, merged 4-bit saves, GGUF exports for llama.cpp, and TorchAO quantization. All methods are dynamically attached to model classes via `patch_saving_functions()`.

---

## 1. Method Registration and Exposure

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2569-2678

### How Methods Are Attached

The `patch_saving_functions()` function dynamically attaches save/export methods to model and tokenizer objects using Python's `types.MethodType()` mechanism:

**For non-vision models** (lines 2658-2668):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**For vision models** (lines 2670-2676):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

**Where it's called:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)

Also wraps the original `push_to_hub` method with `unsloth_push_to_hub` via dynamic code generation (lines 2586-2640).

---

## 2. Main Save Function

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 205-762
**Decorator:** `@torch.inference_mode`

### Signature
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)
```

### Key Features

**Save Methods Supported** (line 283-290):
- `"lora"`: Saves only LoRA adapter weights (fastest)
- `"merged_16bit"`: Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `"merged_4bit"`: Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)

**Memory-Efficient Merged Saving** (lines 483-747):
The function implements a sophisticated memory-aware saving strategy for merged models:

1. **Layer-by-layer LoRA merging** (lines 567-602):
   - Iterates through model layers progressively
   - Uses `_merge_lora()` helper to merge LoRA weights with base weights
   - Saves weights to GPU memory if space available, otherwise disk
   - Implements memory pressure monitoring via `max_vram` and `max_ram`

2. **LoRA Merging Helper** - `_merge_lora()` (lines 159-187):
   - Extracts LoRA parameters: W (weight), A, B (low-rank factors), s (scale), bias
   - Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
   - Computes merged weight via: `W = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))`
   - Returns merged weight and bias

3. **Quantization config removal** (lines 682-693):
   - Strips `quantization_config` from model config before saving merged model
   - Updates all layers' configs

4. **Multi-shard organization upload** (lines 699-721):
   - For org repos: saves locally first, then uploads via `HfApi.upload_folder()`
   - For user repos: directly saves with `push_to_hub=True`

### Return Values
- Returns tuple: `(save_directory, None)` for LoRA/merged_4bit
- Returns tuple: `(save_directory, username)` for push_to_hub merged models

---

## 3. Merged Model Saving

### `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1321-1361
**Type:** Instance method (attached via `types.MethodType`)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... additional HF save_pretrained params
):
    # Simply calls unsloth_save_model with self as model
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    gc.collect()  # 3x
```

### `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1364-1407
**Type:** Instance method

Wrapper that calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

### Generic Versions (for non-Llama architectures)
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 2413-2453): Calls `unsloth_generic_save()`
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 2456-2499): Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` with push

These use `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from unsloth_zoo for non-standard architectures.

---

## 4. GGUF Export Implementation

### `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1768-1949

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,  # Initial dtype: f16, bf16, f32, q8_0
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    # ... standard save params
):
```

**Flow:**
1. **LoRA merging to 16-bit** (lines 1822-1855):
   - First merges LoRA to float16 via `unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")`
   - Installs llama.cpp in parallel if needed (non-blocking)

2. **GGUF conversion** (lines 1898-1902):
   - Calls `save_to_gguf()` with model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece_model, quantization_method
   - Returns `(all_file_locations, want_full_precision)` tuple

3. **Ollama Modelfile creation** (lines 1904-1913):
   - Generates Ollama Modelfile via `create_ollama_modelfile()`
   - Saves to `{save_directory}/Modelfile`

4. **Upload to HF** (lines 2076-2115):
   - Uploads each GGUF file and Modelfile to HuggingFace Hub
   - Skips full-precision version if not in quantization_method list

### Core GGUF Conversion: `save_to_gguf()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 952-1317

```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,  # "float16" or "bfloat16"
    is_sentencepiece: bool = False,
    model_directory: str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method = "fast_quantized",  # Can be list
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer = None,
):
    # Returns: (all_saved_locations, full_precision_seen)
```

**Quantization Presets** (lines 1006-1023):
```python
"not_quantized"  -> model_dtype (f16/bf16/f32)
"fast_quantized" -> "q8_0"
"quantized"      -> "q4_k_m"
```

**Supported Quantization Methods** (ALLOWED_QUANTS dict, lines 79-100):
- f32, bf16, f16 (full precision variants)
- q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q4_k_s, q5_k_s, q2_k, q3_k_*, q4_*, q5_*, q6_k (quantized variants)

**Conversion Process** (lines 1172-1317):

1. **Install llama.cpp** (lines 1048-1114):
   - Looks for existing `llama-quantize` and `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
   - Falls back to blocking install if not found

2. **Determine first_conversion strength** (lines 1116-1170):
   - Calculates required precision level based on quantization methods
   - Validates bf16 support on device

3. **HF to GGUF conversion** (lines 1172-1239):
   - Detects vocab type (spm,hfft,bpe vs bpe)
   - Patches convert script to add Unsloth metadata
   - Handles mistral_common import fallback
   - Executes: `python convert-hf-to-gguf.py {model_dir} --outfile {output} --outtype {dtype}`

4. **Quantization** (lines 1271-1312):
   - For each quantization method (if different from first_conversion):
   - Executes: `./llama-quantize {input} {output} {method} {n_cpus}`
   - Creates output files like: `unsloth.Q4_K_M.gguf`

**Return Value:**
- `all_saved_locations`: List of file paths (full precision first, then quantized variants)
- `full_precision_seen`: Boolean, True if first_conversion is in quantization_method list

### `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`

2. Merges model

### Generic Vision GGUF Export: `save_to_gguf_generic()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`

## 6. TorchAO Quantization

### `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    torchao_config=None,  # TorchAoBaseConfig
    push_to_hub: bool = False,

```

**Flow:**
1. First merges model to float16 via `unsloth_generic_save(..., save_method="merged_16bit")`

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document of all the save/export functionality in unsloth.

## Comprehensive Report: Unsloth Model Save/Export Implementation

### Overview
Unsloth provides multiple ways to save and export fine-tuned models: adapter-only LoRA saves, merged 16-bit saves, merged 4-bit saves, GGUF exports for llama.cpp, and TorchAO quantization. All methods are dynamically attached to model classes via `patch_saving_functions()`.

---

## 1. Method Registration and Exposure

**Function:** `patch_saving_functions()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2569-2678

### How Methods Are Attached

The `patch_saving_functions()` function dynamically attaches save/export methods to model and tokenizer objects using Python's `types.MethodType()` mechanism:

**For non-vision models** (lines 2658-2668):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**For vision models** (lines 2670-2676):
```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

**Where it's called:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)

Also wraps the original `push_to_hub` method with `unsloth_push_to_hub` via dynamic code generation (lines 2586-2640).

---

## 2. Main Save Function

**Function:** `unsloth_save_model()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 205-762
**Decorator:** `@torch.inference_mode`

### Signature
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)
```

### Key Features

**Save Methods Supported** (line 283-290):
- `"lora"`: Saves only LoRA adapter weights (fastest)
- `"merged_16bit"`: Merges LoRA into float16 weights (for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `"merged_4bit"`: Merges LoRA into int4 weights (for DPO/inference)

**Memory-Efficient Merged Saving** (lines 483-747):
The function implements a sophisticated memory-aware saving strategy for merged models:

1. **Layer-by-layer LoRA merging** (lines 567-602):
   - Iterates through model layers progressively
   - Uses `_merge_lora()` helper to merge LoRA weights with base weights
   - Saves weights to GPU memory if space available, otherwise disk
   - Implements memory pressure monitoring via `max_vram` and `max_ram`

2. **LoRA Merging Helper** - `_merge_lora()` (lines 159-187):
   - Extracts LoRA parameters: W (weight), A, B (low-rank factors), s (scale), bias
   - Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
   - Computes merged weight via: `W = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))`
   - Returns merged weight and bias

3. **Quantization config removal** (lines 682-693):
   - Strips `quantization_config` from model config before saving merged model
   - Updates all layers' configs

4. **Multi-shard organization upload** (lines 699-721):
   - For org repos: saves locally first, then uploads via `HfApi.upload_folder()`
   - For user repos: directly saves with `push_to_hub=True`

### Return Values
- Returns tuple: `(save_directory, None)` for LoRA/merged_4bit
- Returns tuple: `(save_directory, username)` for push_to_hub merged models

---

## 3. Merged Model Saving

### `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1321-1361
**Type:** Instance method (attached via `types.MethodType`)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... additional HF save_pretrained params
):
    # Simply calls unsloth_save_model with self as model
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    gc.collect()  # 3x
```

### `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1364-1407
**Type:** Instance method

Wrapper that calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

### Generic Versions (for non-Llama architectures)
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 2413-2453): Calls `unsloth_generic_save()`
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 2456-2499): Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` with push

These use `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from unsloth_zoo for non-standard architectures.

---

## 4. GGUF Export Implementation

### `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1768-1949

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,  # Initial dtype: f16, bf16, f32, q8_0
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    # ... standard save params
):
```

**Flow:**
1. **LoRA merging to 16-bit** (lines 1822-1855):
   - First merges LoRA to float16 via `unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")`
   - Installs llama.cpp in parallel if needed (non-blocking)

2. **GGUF conversion** (lines 1898-1902):
   - Calls `save_to_gguf()` with model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece_model, quantization_method
   - Returns `(all_file_locations, want_full_precision)` tuple

3. **Ollama Modelfile creation** (lines 1904-1913):
   - Generates Ollama Modelfile via `create_ollama_modelfile()`
   - Saves to `{save_directory}/Modelfile`

4. **Upload to HF** (lines 2076-2115):
   - Uploads each GGUF file and Modelfile to HuggingFace Hub
   - Skips full-precision version if not in quantization_method list

### Core GGUF Conversion: `save_to_gguf()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 952-1317

```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,  # "float16" or "bfloat16"
    is_sentencepiece: bool = False,
    model_directory: str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method = "fast_quantized",  # Can be list
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer = None,
):
    # Returns: (all_saved_locations, full_precision_seen)
```

**Quantization Presets** (lines 1006-1023):
```python
"not_quantized"  -> model_dtype (f16/bf16/f32)
"fast_quantized" -> "q8_0"
"quantized"      -> "q4_k_m"
```

**Supported Quantization Methods** (ALLOWED_QUANTS dict, lines 79-100):
- f32, bf16, f16 (full precision variants)
- q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q4_k_s, q5_k_s, q2_k, q3_k_*, q4_*, q5_*, q6_k (quantized variants)

**Conversion Process** (lines 1172-1317):

1. **Install llama.cpp** (lines 1048-1114):
   - Looks for existing `llama-quantize` and `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
   - Falls back to blocking install if not found

2. **Determine first_conversion strength** (lines 1116-1170):
   - Calculates required precision level based on quantization methods
   - Validates bf16 support on device

3. **HF to GGUF conversion** (lines 1172-1239):
   - Detects vocab type (spm,hfft,bpe vs bpe)
   - Patches convert script to add Unsloth metadata
   - Handles mistral_common import fallback
   - Executes: `python convert-hf-to-gguf.py {model_dir} --outfile {output} --outtype {dtype}`

4. **Quantization** (lines 1271-1312):
   - For each quantization method (if different from first_conversion):
   - Executes: `./llama-quantize {input} {output} {method} {n_cpus}`
   - Creates output files like: `unsloth.Q4_K_M.gguf`

**Return Value:**
- `all_saved_locations`: List of file paths (full precision first, then quantized variants)
- `full_precision_seen`: Boolean, True if first_conversion is in quantization_method list

### `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 1951-2125

Parallel version that:
1. Installs llama.cpp in non-blocking background
2. Merges model
3. Converts to GGUF via `save_to_gguf()`
4. Uploads all GGUF files to HF Hub

### Generic Vision GGUF Export: `save_to_gguf_generic()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2276-2352

For vision models and other non-standard architectures:
- Uses `_convert_to_gguf()` from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp
- Supports single or multiple quantization methods
- Uploads to HF via `HfApi.upload_folder()` with `allow_patterns=["*.gguf"]`

---

## 5. LoRA Adapter Saving

### Direct LoRA Save: `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2129-2140

```python
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)
    unsloth_save_model(
        model, tokenizer, 
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )
```

This is used by GGML conversion functions as a prerequisite.

### GGML Conversion: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2143-2209

**Flow:**
1. Installs llama.cpp (blocking, non-blocking per environment)
2. Saves LoRA adapters to `lora-to-ggml-push/` via `save_lora_to_custom_dir()`
3. Runs: `python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_dir} {output_file} llama`
4. Uploads GGML file to HF Hub via `upload_to_huggingface()`

### GGML Local Save: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395850377-3uewgn/unsloth/save.py`
**Lines:** 2211-2262

Same as above but saves locally instead of pushing to HF.

LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames

del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",

{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():

pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
